findings.message: The dirty-worktree handling conflicts with repo policy. This task says implementation work must be "committed or stashed" before writing, but the repo runbook explicitly forbids `git stash` mutations. The plan should require commit-or-wait/coordinate, not stash.

TASK-02's TC-05 is a false validation gate. It greps `AuthenticatedApp.tsx` for `bg-gradient-to-b from-[var(--color-bg)] to-surface-1`, but the current component actually uses `bg-gradient-to-b from-surface to-surface-1` at `apps/reception/src/components/AuthenticatedApp.tsx:23`. This check already passes before any change, so it does not validate gradient removal.

Removing `AuthenticatedApp`'s global `p-6` is treated mostly as a `/bar` risk, but `AuthenticatedApp` wraps the entire authenticated app in `apps/reception/src/App.tsx:157-164`. The plan itself says 12 staff-visible routes bypass `PageShell`, so this change has broader blast radius than the single bar smoke check acknowledges.

The plan overstates regression coverage with "CI parity tests pass for all affected routes." The actual parity suite only covers five routes under `apps/reception/src/parity/__tests__` (`/checkin`, `/checkout`, `/safe-management`, `/till-reconciliation`, and unauthenticated `/bar`). That is not enough evidence for a global shell change touching all authenticated screens and 17 `PageShell` consumers.
